title: RestoreFleetManifest
description: Single source of truth for the IGLA Railway fleet shape. Consumed by `tri railway restore` (RW-DR-01) to rebuild from a clean account after a ban. Versioned via top-level `version`. Anchor: phi^2 + phi^-2 = 3. Refs #25, #26, #28.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema for editor autocomplete.

version.description: Manifest schema version. Bump with breaking changes.

anchor.description: Constitutional anchor. Cosmetic; readers must not rely on its value.

name.description: Railway project name to create or reuse.

description.description: Optional human-readable project description shown in the Railway UI.

default_environment.description: Environment name to upsert variables and trigger deploys against.

image.description: Container image used by every service unless `services[].image_override` is set. Either a bare reference (resolved to `:latest` at restore time) or a fully-qualified pin including a tag or `@sha256:` digest.

shared_vars.description: Variables upserted on every service before its first deploy. Use `${secret:NAME}` to reference an env-var the operator must provide at restore time (resolved by `tri railway restore`, never written to git).

services.description: List of services to create. Order is preserved; duplicates by `name` are forbidden.

post_restore.description: Optional automation hooks executed after every service has been deployed.

neon_ddl.description: Shell command piped into psql against $NEON_DATABASE_URL. Recommended: `tri-railway audit migrate-sql`.

embargo_check.description: Shell command that exits 0 if the embargo SHA list is intact. Refs R9.

experience_line.description: Template for the L7 experience triplet sealed after restore. Supports `${project_uuid_8c}`, `${git_sha_8c}`, `${rfc3339}` placeholders.

value.description: Literal value, or `${secret:NAME}` to interpolate from process env / GitHub Secrets at restore time. Secrets are NEVER written to git.

name.description: Service name as it will appear in Railway. Must match `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,62}$` to satisfy Railway DNS rules.

kind.description: Free-form classifier consumed by tooling. Common values: `trainer`, `mcp`, `agent`, `sidecar`.

image_override.description: Per-service override of the top-level `image`. Same format rules.

vars.description: Variables specific to this service. Merged on top of `shared_vars`; per-service keys win on conflict.
